item.title: Associahedron
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)

item.title: Herb
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)
item.description: From an amazon.com review of Lewis & Papadimitrious’s Elements of the Theory of Computation: And also… who the hell is this geek on the cover? It doesn’t reveal his identity anywhere in the book. You know this book is bad when they have a herb like him staring at you. This guy needs a new suit, a haircut, and he needs to learn how to smile. The herb

item.title: Latin Wiphala
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)
item.description: I’ve been playing with Latin squares today. Here is a wiphala arranged in the pattern of the 7 by 7 Latin square used on the cover of Ronald Fisher’s The Design of Experiments.

item.title: Haskell Akiyama–Tanigawa
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)
item.description: I am a big fan of the Akiyama–Tanigawa algorithm12 for computing Bernoulli numbers. It works by generating a structure resembling Pascal’s triangle, with rows whose entries are determined by a simple calculation on the two entries above them, from which we can read off the sequence of Bernoulli numbers. What I like about this algorithm, besides its simplicity and elegance, is that it admits a concordantly simple and elgant implementation in Haskell using infinite data structures.

item.title: Books 2015 –
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)
item.description: All the books I read since 2015. I also have a wishlist. Rubric Re-reads are marked with an asterisk (∗). Works read in translation are marked with a dagger (†). Things I read for book club are marked with a tilde (~). 2023 You Are Not Expected to Understand This, Torie Bosch (ed.). This Is How You Lose the Time War, Amal El-Mohtar & Max Gladstone. Gone: A Search for what Remains of the World’s Extinct Creatures, Michael Blencowe.

item.title: Maths
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)
item.description: I used to do research in pure maths. Here is the proof. Publications Binary Complexes and Algebraic K-theory. (PhD thesis) Southampton ePrints Algebraic proofs of some fundamental theorems in algebraic K-theory. arXiv, published Exterior power operations on higher K-groups via binary complexes. (with Bernhard Köck and Lenny Taelman) arXiv, published Two Formulae for exterior power operations on higher K-groups. (with Bernhard Köck) arXiv, published Other Some notes on the Pisano semiperiod.

item.title: Wishlist
item.author: tkmharris@protonmail.com (Tom Harris)
item.description: Some books I’d like, in case you need to get me a gift. Ælfred’s Britain: War and Peace in the Viking Age, Max Adams. At the Existentialist Cafe, Sarah Bakewell. The Age of Surveillance Capitalism, Shoshana Zuboff. The Age of Wonder, Richard Holmes. All the Wrong Moves, Sasha Chapin. The Anomoly, Hervé le Tellier. The Ashtray: (Or the Man Who Denied Reality), Errol Morris. Backroom Boys, Francis Spufford. The Beauty of Everyday Things, Soetsu Yanagi.
